articles.headline: The Organ Science Just Stopped Ignoring (And It Affects How Long You Live)
articles.body: There is a small organ behind your breastbone called the thymus. Doctors wrote it off decades ago. Big mistake. Harvard researchers used AI to analyze CT scans from over 25,000 adults and found that people with a healthier thymus had roughly 50 percent lower risk of death, a 63 percent lower risk of dying from heart disease, and a 36 percent lower risk of developing lung cancer. The thymus trains your immune T-cells, and it turns out it keeps working well into adulthood. Chronic inflammation and high body weight are both linked to poorer thymic health. Two studies. Both published in Nature. Hard to ignore.
articles.takeaway: Keep inflammation low and body weight in check. It protects more than you think.

articles.headline: Deep Sleep Is Secretly Your Best Gym Session
articles.body: UC Berkeley researchers just cracked something big. They mapped the brain circuits that control growth hormone release during sleep and found that deep, non-REM sleep triggers a powerful hormone surge that builds muscle, burns fat, strengthens bones, and sharpens mental focus. Cut that deep sleep short and growth hormone drops. The researchers also found a feedback loop where growth hormone nudges the brain toward waking, which means the balance is delicate. Skimp on sleep and you are not just tired. You are actively short-changing your recovery, your metabolism, and your brain clarity the next day.
articles.takeaway: Prioritize deep sleep like a training session. It is literally where your gains are made.

product.name: LectroFan White Noise Machine
product.blurb: Blocks ambient noise so your sleep is actually restorative.

articles.headline: Structured Workouts Beat Random Movement for Better Sleep
articles.body: A large-scale study of over 700,000 Japanese adults found that starting a structured exercise routine was linked to 37 percent higher odds of better sleep quality. Planned, intentional workouts had a far stronger effect than general daily movement like step counts. Maintaining an existing exercise habit still gave a 23 percent sleep boost even for people who were already active but sleeping poorly. The mechanism is real: regular structured exercise regulates your stress hormones and helps your brain downshift into restorative sleep. The weekend warrior approach does not cut it here. Frequency and intention matter.
articles.takeaway: Schedule your workouts like meetings. Consistency is the actual sleep supplement.

product.name: Garmin Forerunner 255
product.blurb: The watch Chris trains with daily. Tracks every discipline without breaking the bank.

articles.headline: The Government Just Raised the Bar on How Much Protein You Should Eat
articles.body: The new 2025 to 2030 US Dietary Guidelines officially raised the recommended protein target for adults. The new guidance calls for 1.2 to 1.6 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight per day. For a 150-pound person, that is 82 to 109 grams daily. The previous recommendation was just 0.8 grams per kilogram. The guidelines also now say protein should be prioritized at every single meal, not just dinner. Older adults and anyone trying to manage weight or build muscle have the most to gain. The science has been pointing this direction for years. The official guidance finally caught up.
articles.takeaway: Aim for at least 30 grams of protein at breakfast. Most people fall way short early in the day.

quote.text: Take care of your body. It's the only place you have to live.
quote.author: Jim Rohn
quote.context: American entrepreneur, author, and motivational speaker who influenced a generation of performance coaches.

workout.title: Reset and Rebuild: Full-Body Sunday Flow
workout.warmup: Start with 2 minutes of slow, deep diaphragmatic breathing lying flat on your back. Then do 60 seconds of gentle neck rolls and shoulder circles to wake up the joints without any loading. Finish with a slow cat-cow sequence on all fours, 8 reps, matching your breath to each movement.

exercises.name: Foam Roll Thoracic Spine
exercises.notes: Place the roller perpendicular to your spine at mid-back. Support your head with both hands, drop your hips to the floor, and slowly roll from mid-back to upper back. Pause on any tight spots for a breath or two. Keep your core soft.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
exercises.notes: Sit with both knees bent at 90 degrees, one in front and one to the side. Keep your chest tall and hinge forward slightly over the front shin. This unlocks tight hips from long hours of sitting. Switch sides and repeat.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.notes: From a lunge position, place same-side hand inside your front foot. Rotate your top arm up toward the ceiling, following it with your eyes. Slow and controlled. Hits hip flexors, thoracic rotation, and hamstrings in one move.

exercises.name: Supine Piriformis Stretch (Figure Four)
exercises.notes: Lie on your back, cross one ankle over the opposite knee, and gently pull the uncrossed leg toward your chest. Breathe into the stretch. If you sit at a desk, your piriformis is almost certainly tight. Take your time here.

exercises.name: Child's Pose to Downward Dog Flow
exercises.notes: From child's pose, press up through your hands into downward dog, pedaling your heels gently. Hold downward dog for 3 breaths, then flow back to child's pose. This restores length to the entire posterior chain.

workout.cooldown: Finish with 3 to 5 minutes in savasana, flat on your back with arms slightly away from your sides. Let your breath return to its natural rhythm. This is not optional. The nervous system needs a clear signal that the session is done.

product.name: TriggerPoint GRID Foam Roller
product.blurb: Five minutes of rolling saves hours of stiffness. Non-negotiable on hard training days.

meal.title: Greek Chicken Power Bowl
ingredients: 1/2 cup canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed
meal.instructions: 1. If using leftover or rotisserie chicken, slice or shred and season with oregano, salt, and pepper. 2. Layer brown rice or farro in the base of a bowl, then add chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion. 3. Top with sliced chicken and crumbled feta. 4. Mix Greek yogurt with olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper, then drizzle over the bowl as a dressing.
meal.tip: Batch-cook a large portion of chicken and grains on Sunday and you have lunch ready for three days with zero effort. Swap farro for rice or quinoa depending on what you have.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
